metrics.narrative: No quantified business problem, cost savings target, cycle-time improvement, or ROI case has been articulated by the buyer or documented in Salesforce. The NextStep history (25 Jun, 15 Jun, 21 May, 27 Apr) repeatedly references the need to define and validate 'a clear business case' and 'the most relevant B Case' to present to CPO Emmanuelle Wallon and 'convince the family Clarins (Family Courtin-Clarins owning Clarins)', but no specific pain points, financial impact, or quantified value drivers have been captured. Alice Levan (champion) is tasked with defining these internally before the July prep meeting (NextStep 25 Jun: 'they need first to define internally the best business case(s) / use case(s) that would convince Emmanuelle'). The absence of a quantified value case at 92 days into the cycle, with a September CPO meeting on the horizon, is a significant execution risk: a weak or non-quantified business case will not survive CPO and family-ownership scrutiny. The immediate move is to co-build a quantified business case with Alice and Marilyn Bordais in the July prep meeting, anchoring on procurement savings by category, AI-driven efficiency (Merlin), and contract/supplier risk reduction, and to validate that case lands with the CPO before the RFP is released.

champion.narrative: Alice Levan, Global Sustainable & POS Procurement Manager (Evaluator contact role), is the confirmed and active champion. She has been the primary point of contact since the deal's inception (NextStep 31 Mar: 'John to chase Alice Levan to confirm first meeting'), coordinated the on-site demo with CPO Emmanuelle Wallon on 23 April 2026, led the follow-up discussion on next steps and timeline on 27 April, organized the attendance of 4 Clarins procurement employees at the Zycus AI Paris Tour on 16 June (NextStep 21 May: 'So clearly we have not only Alice as champion but also the key procurement people'), and co-led the post-event debrief meeting on 15 June (NextStep: 'Good meeting during HA days need to have a meeting with Marylin and Alice'). She has access to the CPO (Emmanuelle Wallon) and is tasked with defining the business case internally and scheduling the July prep meeting and September CPO meeting (NextStep 25 Jun: 'Alice should come back to me but they need first to define internally the best business case(s) / use case(s) that would convince Emmanuelle'). Alice is multi-threading within procurement: she is coordinating with Marilyn Bordais (Media & Marketing Services Procurement Manager) on business-case definition and use-case prioritization (NextStep 15 Jun: 'need to have a meeting with Marylin and Alice'). Her advocacy is demonstrated by her expanding the engagement to include key procurement peers (Mickael Sarkissian IT/Digital/CAPEX, Julie Belin Declerck Direct Procurement, Remi Belle Finance & HR DPO, Antoine Saouma CSR per contact roles) and by driving Clarins attendance at the Zycus AI event, which is a proactive move to educate and build internal buy-in. However, Alice's championship is currently process-gated: she owns the internal business-case definition and the July meeting is awaiting her revert (NextStep 25 Jun: 'To chase Alice if no news by 7th of July'), which introduces a dependency. Her engagement is active (last contact 25 Jun, 6 days ago), but the strength of her advocacy will be tested by whether the business case she builds is compelling enough to secure CPO sponsorship in September and win family-level approval. The move is to ensure the July prep meeting happens by 7 July, co-build a quantified business case with Alice and Marilyn, and validate Alice's confidence that the case will land with Emmanuelle and the Courtin-Clarins family before the RFP is released.

competition.narrative: SAP Ariba is the confirmed competitor (Salesforce Competitors__c field). No other vendors have been named in NextStep or Salesforce fields, and no competitive intelligence from calls, tasks, or Avoma transcripts is available (no transcribed buyer calls discovered for this opportunity). The buyer's stated RFP timeline (Q3/Q4 2026 release, NextStep 27 Apr and 21 May) and the presence of Ariba as the named competitor suggest Clarins is running a competitive evaluation and benchmarking Zycus against the market leader. However, the nature of the competitive threat is unclear: it is unknown whether Clarins currently uses Ariba (replacement scenario), is benchmarking Ariba as the incumbent/default choice, or is evaluating Ariba and Zycus as two of several shortlisted vendors. No 'Replacing_What__c', 'Existing_vendor__c', 'How_are_you_addressing_your_problem_toda__c', or 'Why_not_Zycus__c' / 'Zycus_Differentiation_Why_Zycus__c' fields are populated to clarify current state or Zycus positioning vs Ariba. The attendance of 4 Clarins procurement employees at the Zycus AI Paris Tour on 16 June (NextStep 21 May) signals Zycus has an AI/Merlin differentiation angle that resonated enough to pull a cross-functional team to the event, and the comprehensive product scope (Merlin, iRisk, Merlin Intake, ANA alongside core S2P modules) suggests Zycus is positioned on AI-driven intelligence and risk management vs Ariba's scale and ERP integration. The wedge is likely Merlin's AI/negotiation/analytics capability and Zycus' procurement-native risk and intelligence platform vs Ariba's broader but less AI-differentiated suite. However, this wedge has not been tested with the CPO (last engaged 23 Apr, before the AI Paris Tour) and is not yet embedded in the business case Alice is building. The move is to use the July business-case prep meeting to position Merlin and iRisk/ANA as the differentiators, co-build use cases that play to the relevant stakeholder and risk strengths (e.g. AI-driven contract risk scoring, Merlin-powered supplier negotiation, category spend intelligence), and validate that wedge lands with Alice and Marilyn before presenting to the CPO in September. If Ariba is the incumbent or default, the business case must articulate why Zycus' AI and intelligence justify switching cost; if Ariba is a benchmarking competitor, the case must show Zycus delivers step-change value Ariba cannot match.

The deal was created 31 March 2026 and has progressed through initial meetings, an on-site demo with the CPO, and a Zycus AI event, but the NextStep history contains no buyer quotes describing current-state problems, procurement inefficiencies, contract risk, supplier performance issues, spend visibility gaps, or compliance challenges. The NextStep references the need to define 'a clear business case' and 'the most relevant B Case' (25 Jun, 15 Jun, 21 May) to present to the CPO and family ownership, but the underlying pain that business case must address is unstated. The comprehensive product scope (iContract, iSource, iSupplier, eProcurement, eInvoicing, iRequest, Merlin, iRisk, Merlin Intake, ANA) suggests the buyer is seeking end-to-end S2P transformation, and the attendance of 4 Clarins procurement employees at the Zycus AI Paris Tour (16 Jun) signals interest in AI-driven procurement and automation, implying pain around manual processes, lack of AI/intelligence, or inefficient workflows. However, these are inferences, not confirmed pain. The competitor is SAP Ariba (Salesforce Competitors__c), which may indicate Clarins is benchmarking against a market leader or considering a replacement, but no 'Replacing_What__c' or 'Pain_points_in_the_current_solution__c' fields are populated. The immediate move is to use the July business-case prep meeting to co-discover and document the 1-2 highest-impact pain points (e.g. lack of spend visibility by category, manual contract risk management, slow procurement cycle time, limited AI/automation) with Alice Levan and Marilyn Bordais, quantify the impact, and anchor the business case on solving those pains. Without confirmed pain, the business case risks being a feature comparison rather than a compelling change story.

items.action: Use the September CPO meeting to convert Emmanuelle Wallon from passive approver to active sponsor: present the quantified business case co-built with Alice and Marilyn, validate it resonates with her priorities, confirm the RFP timeline and selection process (Q3/Q4 2026 RFP release, Q1 2027 selection), and map the family Courtin-Clarins approval chain (who, criteria, timing) so the post-selection path to close is clear.

flags: AI differentiation is the wedge but not yet embedded in a buyer-validated business case. Four Clarins procurement employees attended the AI Paris Tour on 16 Jun (~10 days ago), signaling AI appetite, and the product scope includes Merlin agentic modules (negotiation, intake, risk agents). However, no business case has been co-developed, and the buyer is still defining use cases internally (per 25 Jun Next Step). The business-case prep meeting targeted for third week of July (~3 weeks out) is the gate to translate AI interest into a quantified, CPO-ready value story that positions Merlin as the Phase 1 centerpiece and contrasts Ariba's traditional automation with Zycus's autonomous agents.

Champion Alice Levan is strong and multi-threading (4 procurement attendees at AI event 16 Jun, prep meeting planned with Marilyn for third week of July), but she is an Evaluator, not a Decision Maker or Economic Buyer. Her ability to advocate to the CPO and ownership depends on the strength of the business case. Equip her with a quantified ROI model, reference customers in luxury/beauty, and a crisp AI differentiation story so she can carry the message upward with confidence.

Co-develop quantified business case with champion Alice Levan and Marilyn Bordais in the Jul prep meeting (target third week of Jul, ~3 weeks out): anchor Merlin AI sourcing/negotiation agent on procurement cycle-time reduction, spend under management, or headcount efficiency to differentiate vs SAP Ariba before RFP release (Q3/Q4 2026).

Expand multi-thread to finance and legal stakeholders ahead of RFP (Q3/Q4 2026): Remi Belle (Finance & HR DPO) attended the 23 Apr demo but finance/budget authority is not yet engaged; map legal and InfoSec contacts before RFP to de-risk contracting phase (Q1 2027).

Co-create quantified business case with Alice Levan and Marilyn Bordais in mid-Jul prep meeting (target week of 15 Jul, ~2 weeks out): the business case is the gate to CPO sponsorship and the RFP, and it must land with CPO Emmanuelle Wallon in Sep and withstand Courtin-Clarins family scrutiny. Anchor it to specific Clarins procurement pain (cost, cycle time, risk, compliance, AI adoption), quantify the ROI (savings, automation, headcount leverage), and position Merlin agentic AI (negotiation agent, sourcing intelligence) as the capability Ariba cannot match.

Map the post-RFP process and contracting cycle time in mid-Jul session with Alice: ask what the post-selection process looks like (legal, procurement, InfoSec, who owns it, typical cycle time from selection to signature) so the close-date forecast (19 Mar 2027, 262 days out) can be stress-tested against the buyer timeline (RFP Q3/Q4, selection Q1 2027). If contracting typically takes 10-12 weeks post-selection, a Q1-end selection leaves no buffer for the 19 Mar close, and the date should slip to Q2 2027.
Win the CPO gate: co-build one to two NUMBERS-backed use cases (savings by category) with Alice and Marilyn so the early-September CPO presentation lands and converts Emmanuelle Wallon to sponsor.

Shape the RFP before it opens: pre-position Agentic AI / Merlin Intake and CSR/ESG steering as must-have criteria now, while there is no live bake-off, to disadvantage the likely SAP Ariba default at Q3/Q4 RFP.

Map above the CPO: the Courtin-Clarins family ownership is named as the final approval layer but unmapped - use Alice to identify who in the family/exec layer must back funding and what they care about.
Convert the proposed September CPO meeting from idea to scheduled by first landing a quantified business case Alice can carry to Emmanuelle Wallon.
Map the Courtin-Clarins family-ownership approval layer now; it is named as final authority (21 May 2026) but the chain beyond the CPO is unknown.
Build a quantified savings/ROI use case (savings tracking by category) before September; a qualitative-only case will not survive CPO and family scrutiny vs SAP Ariba or do-nothing.

hard.ais_why:
• Sole Avoma meeting (23 Apr 2026) is explicitly titled 'Zycus Agentic AI x Clarins' ‚Äî the meeting title is itself the strongest possible buyer-AI-interest signal. Recording was disabled (missing conferencing details) so no transcript-level notes are available, but the title plus 4 Clarins attendees (Alice Levan, Emmanuelle Wallon, Remi Belle, plus the opp owner) confirms AI-focused dialogue was the meeting's purpose.
• Qualified stage at $200K. Product scope has ANA, Merlin and Merlin Intake plus a broad S2P suite (eInvoicing, eProcurement, iContract, iRequest, iRisk, iSource, iSupplier). BD_Engagement is Unengaged in SFDC but LastActivity 2026-04-24 indicates recency. Multiple named stakeholders engaged. The opp is a major French luxury cosmetics buyer; AI in luxury procurement is a strategic positioning play.
• Medium confidence: the explicit 'Zycus Agentic AI x Clarins' meeting title and stakeholder breadth support AI Hungry status, but the absence of transcript-level evidence prevents High confidence. Score 7 reflects the AI-titled meeting + 3 AI SKUs in scope + multi-stakeholder attendance; would push to 8-9 with a transcript confirming buyer-spoken AI use cases.

Economic Buyer CPO Emmanuelle Wallon has not been engaged; meeting proposed for September 2026 (~3 months out), gated by internal business-case definition. Courtin-Clarins family ownership noted as final approval layer, but approval chain not mapped.
No quantified business problem or ROI case; buyer defining internally. Business-case strength must withstand CPO and family ownership scrutiny.
RFP release timing (Q3/Q4 2026) and selection (Q1 2027) are buyer-stated but not formally validated. Slippage in RFP release or internal approval could push close date.
No pricing or commercials discussion has occurred; deal is pre-RFP. Pricing will emerge during RFP response and negotiation phase (post-Q3/Q4 2026).

AI differentiation is the wedge but not yet embedded in a buyer-validated business case. Four Clarins procurement employees attended the AI Paris Tour on 16 Jun (~10 days ago), signaling AI appetite, and the product scope includes Merlin agentic modules (negotiation, intake, risk agents). However, no business case has been co-developed, and the buyer is still defining use cases internally (per 25 Jun Next Step). The business-case prep meeting targeted for third week of July (~3 weeks out) is the gate to translate AI interest into a quantified, CPO-ready value story that positions Merlin as the Phase 1 centerpiece and contrasts Ariba's traditional automation with Zycus's autonomous agents.

Economic Buyer Emmanuelle Wallon (CPO, Decision Maker per Contact Roles) has not been engaged. Meeting proposed for September 2026 (~3 months out), gated by internal business-case definition. The Courtin-Clarins family ownership is noted as a final approval layer (21 May), but the approval chain has not been mapped. Before the RFP release (Q3/Q4 2026), validate the CPO's budget authority, understand the family's decision role, and secure early executive sponsorship to de-risk the selection phase.

Champion Alice Levan is strong and multi-threading (4 procurement attendees at AI event 16 Jun, prep meeting planned with Marilyn for third week of July), but she is an Evaluator, not a Decision Maker or Economic Buyer. Her ability to advocate to the CPO and ownership depends on the strength of the business case. Equip her with a quantified ROI model, reference customers in luxury/beauty, and a crisp AI differentiation story so she can carry the message upward with confidence.

CPO Emmanuelle Wallon not engaged since on-site demo on 23 Apr 2026 (~9 weeks ago); re-engagement planned for Sep 2026 (~3 months out), gated by business-case strength. Risk: if the business case does not land strongly with CPO, the Sep meeting may not convert to sponsorship, delaying RFP or weakening Zycus positioning vs Ariba.

No quantified business problem or ROI case defined yet; buyer (Alice Levan) defining internally. Risk: weak business case will not convince CPO or Courtin-Clarins family ownership, reducing win probability or delaying RFP.

Buyer-stated RFP timeline (Q3/Q4 2026, selection Q1 2027) is labeled 'Need to be validated that's the ideal planning' by the buyer (NextStep 27 Apr). Risk: slippage in RFP release or internal approval could push close date beyond 19 Mar 2027.

Courtin-Clarins family ownership layer is referenced as final approval authority, but the approval chain beyond CPO Emmanuelle Wallon is unmapped. Risk: unknown family-level gates or priorities could emerge late and block or delay close.

Co-develop quantified business case with champion Alice Levan and Marilyn Bordais in the Jul prep meeting (target third week of Jul, ~3 weeks out): anchor Merlin AI sourcing/negotiation agent on procurement cycle-time reduction, spend under management, or headcount efficiency to differentiate vs SAP Ariba before RFP release (Q3/Q4 2026).

Re-engage CPO Emmanuelle Wallon in Sep 2026 (~3 months out) with the validated business case: she attended the 23 Apr demo but has not been engaged since; the Sep meeting is the gate to her sponsorship and the path to convincing the Courtin-Clarins family ownership layer.

Expand multi-thread to finance and legal stakeholders ahead of RFP (Q3/Q4 2026): Remi Belle (Finance & HR DPO) attended the 23 Apr demo but finance/budget authority is not yet engaged; map legal and InfoSec contacts before RFP to de-risk contracting phase (Q1 2027).

Economic Buyer CPO Emmanuelle Wallon has not been engaged since on-site demo on 23 Apr 2026 (10 weeks ago); re-engagement planned for Sep 2026 (~2 months out), gated by business-case strength. Risk: if the business case does not land strongly with CPO, the Sep meeting may not convert to sponsorship, delaying RFP or weakening Zycus positioning vs Ariba.

Co-create quantified business case with Alice Levan and Marilyn Bordais in mid-Jul prep meeting (target week of 15 Jul, ~2 weeks out): the business case is the gate to CPO sponsorship and the RFP, and it must land with CPO Emmanuelle Wallon in Sep and withstand Courtin-Clarins family scrutiny. Anchor it to specific Clarins procurement pain (cost, cycle time, risk, compliance, AI adoption), quantify the ROI (savings, automation, headcount leverage), and position Merlin agentic AI (negotiation agent, sourcing intelligence) as the capability Ariba cannot match.

Convert Sep CPO meeting from approval gate to active sponsorship: CPO Emmanuelle Wallon attended the on-site demo on 23 Apr but has not re-engaged since (10 weeks ago). The Sep meeting (proposed 25 Jun, ~2 months out) is conditional on business-case strength. Use the mid-Jul prep session to ensure the business case is CPO-grade, then make the Sep meeting a Merlin AI showcase (live demo of negotiation agent on supplier data, sourcing intelligence on Clarins spend categories) so Emmanuelle becomes an active sponsor, not just an approver.

Map the post-RFP process and contracting cycle time in mid-Jul session with Alice: ask what the post-selection process looks like (legal, procurement, InfoSec, who owns it, typical cycle time from selection to signature) so the close-date forecast (19 Mar 2027, 262 days out) can be stress-tested against the buyer timeline (RFP Q3/Q4, selection Q1 2027). If contracting typically takes 10-12 weeks post-selection, a Q1-end selection leaves no buffer for the 19 Mar close, and the date should slip to Q2 2027.
